member.name: F:Intersect.Server.Core.LogicService.LogicThread.LogicLock
member.summary: We lock on this in order to stop maps from entering the update queue. This is only done when the editor is saving/modifying game maps or the map grids are being rebuilt.

member.name: F:Intersect.Server.Core.LogicService.LogicThread.MapInstanceUpdateQueue
member.summary: Queue of active maps which maps are added to after being updated. Once a map makes it to the front of the queue they are updated again.

member.name: F:Intersect.Server.Core.LogicService.LogicThread.MapInstanceProjectileQueue
member.summary: Queue of active maps which maps are added to after being updated. Once a map makes it to the front of the queue they are updated again. This queue is only used for projectile updates if the projectile update interval does not match the map update interval in the server config.

member.name: F:Intersect.Server.Core.LogicService.LogicThread.ActiveMapInstances
member.summary: This is the set of maps determined to be 'active' based on player locations in the game. Our logic recalculates this hashset every 250ms. When maps are updated they are not added back into the map update queues unless they exist in this hash set.

member.name: M:Intersect.Server.Core.LogicService.LogicThread.AddToQueue(Intersect.Server.Maps.MapInstance)
member.summary: Adds a map instance to the map update queues for our logic loop to start processing.
member.param: The map instance in which to process in our queues.

member.name: M:Intersect.Server.Core.LogicService.LogicThread.UpdateMap(Intersect.Server.Maps.MapInstance,System.Boolean)
member.summary: This function actually runs our map update function on the logic thread pool, and then re-queues our map for future updates if the map is still considered active.
member.param: The our thread updates.
If true only map projectiles are updated and not the entire map.

member.summary: Adjusts the position of a projectile when it crosses the boundaries of the current map. This method checks if the projectile crosses the map boundaries and, if so, attempts to transition the projectile to the adjacent map in the direction it is moving. The new position on the adjacent map is calculated to maintain the continuity of the projectile's path.

member.name: T:Intersect.Server.Maps.MapController
member.summary:
A exists to provide a reference to some and it's neighbors, as well as give hooks to the Instance's list of .
A Map Controller is generally not responsible for sending packets to players, that is the job of the Instances, stored in . However, the exception to that case is whenever we want to perform some action for ALL players on a map, regardless of whatever Instance they are currently on. In those cases, that logic should live here, and generally means iterating over the map instance that belong to this .
A good example of this can be seen in the Respawn/Despawning functions of a - these functions are called when we update a Map from the editor, or otherwise need to refresh what a looks like under the hood, and the methods go out to each instance to refresh the packets that are being sent to the player/instances that are being processed by the .

member.name: T:Intersect.Server.Maps.MapInstance
member.summary:
A exists to process map updates, but on a "layered" system, where a single map can be processing differently for each instance that exists upon it.
A contains a list , each one responsible for sending the necessary packet updates to the players that exist on that instance.
By taking the processing out of the itself, we can support map "Instancing" - ergo, the ability to process the same map differently for different players/parties. This allows for concepts such as dungeons and personal cutscenes, minigames, duel arenas, etc etc.
A is the sole creator of MapInstances. A new instance will be created (via a call to ) when a player:
Warps to a new map, or their has otherwise changed.
Walks across a map boundary and fetches new maps from .
A needs to know when it is ready to be cleaned up, if its instance ID is not that of the overworld. A MapController can be marked for cleanup if there are no players on itself or any of its surrounding layers, and at least ms have passed since the last player was on the map/its neighbors.

member.name: F:Intersect.Server.Maps.MapInstance.OverworldInstanceId
member.summary: Reference to stay consistent/easy-to-read with overworld behavior

A unique identifier referring to this processing instance alone.
Note that this is NOT the Instance instance identifier - that is

member.name: P:Intersect.Server.Maps.MapInstance.MapInstanceId
member.summary:
An ID referring to which instance this processer belongs to.
Entities/Items/Etc with that share an with will be processed and fed packets by that processer.

member.name: F:Intersect.Server.Maps.MapInstance.LastRequestedUpdateTime
member.summary:
The last time the made a call to .
This is used to determine when enough time has passed to cleanup this instance (remove it from it's parent ).

member.name: M:Intersect.Server.Maps.MapInstance.Initialize
member.summary: Initializes the processing instance for processing - called in the constructor. Essentially refreshes the instance so that it can give everything it has to offer to the player by the time they arrive in it

member.name: M:Intersect.Server.Maps.MapInstance.GetControllerLock
member.summary: Gets our _parent's_ map lock.
member.returns: The parent map instance's map lock

member.name: M:Intersect.Server.Maps.MapInstance.DespawnEverything
member.summary: Destroys the processable elements of the instance - RespawnEverything() to bring them back, and begin processing them in the update loop once more

member.name: M:Intersect.Server.Maps.MapInstance.RespawnEverything
member.summary: Respawns everything - if this isn't called, the map will never begin processing anything in it's update loop, as it will have nothing to process. Note this also refreshes the event cache - refreshing events, global and local alike, when an Instance is refreshed.

member.name: M:Intersect.Server.Maps.MapInstance.Update(System.Int64)
member.summary: The update loop - runs everytime the job in the LogicThread class executes it. Takes care of: - Checking if items need to spawn/be despawned - Move/process entity actions, regen - Determine if NPCs need respawned/regen'd - Determine if Resources need respawned/regen'd - Update global event processing
member.param: The current time the update was called. Used for determining updates to things such as respawn timers.

member.name: M:Intersect.Server.Maps.MapInstance.AddEntity(Intersect.Server.Entities.Entity)
member.summary: Adds an entity to the MapInstance so that the instance knows to process them.
member.param: The entity to add.

member.name: M:Intersect.Server.Maps.MapInstance.RemoveEntity(Intersect.Server.Entities.Entity)
member.summary: Removes an entity. After removal, that entity will no longer be updated by the processing instance. If this entity is a player, they will experience a complete desync from the server, as the map would not be sending them update packets any longer - so be mindful of what you're removing and when.
member.param: The entity to remove.
